name: Rowan Atkinson
blurb: The face is the bit. Before Bean, before Blackadder, he was a solo stand-up — the Schoolmaster, the Devil welcoming you to Hell, the Invisible Drum Kit — building character monologues that turned a single performer into an entire revue.
bio: Rowan Atkinson is best known for Mr. Bean and Blackadder, but his foundation is solo live comedy: Edinburgh Fringe in the late 1970s, Oxford and London revues with Richard Curtis and Howard Goodall, then a four-month UK tour in 1980 that produced the Live in Belfast album. Throughout the 1980s he wrote and toured solo shows with Curtis and Ben Elton — character monologues like the cane-twirling Schoolmaster taking roll, the bored Devil 'Toby' welcoming the damned ('Christians! Christians over here, please…'), the Conductor and the Invisible Drum Kit — work that was anthologised in the 1992 TV special Rowan Atkinson Live and shipped, alongside Live in Belfast (1980) and Not Just a Pretty Face (1987), as his core stand-up record. The character bits feed straight into Mr. Bean (1990) and Blackadder, but the live solo work is its own world: there is no British comic in his generation whose face does more of the work alone on stage.

timeline.ev: Born January 6 in Consett, County Durham, England
Edinburgh Festival Fringe debut at the Oxford Theatre Group's revue
Performs the Schoolmaster sketch at The Secret Policeman's Ball after Cleese spots him
Not the Nine O'Clock News begins on BBC (runs through 1982)
Four-month UK solo tour with Richard Curtis and Howard Goodall; recorded as Live in Belfast at the Grand Opera House
UK solo tour with Angus Deayton as straight man; appears at Just For Laughs Montreal 1987 and 1989
Mr. Bean debuts on ITV (runs through 1995)
Second major solo tour, again with Deayton — anthologised in the 1992 TV special
Rowan Atkinson Live TV special compiles 1981–1986 stand-up tour material

accolades: BAFTA — Best Light Entertainment Performance, Blackadder Goes Forth (1990)
CableACE nomination — Best Comedy Special, Rowan Atkinson Live (1993)
Perrier Comedy Award winner — Oxford Theatre Group revue, Edinburgh Fringe (1976)
Mr. Bean: official YouTube channels have surpassed 70+ million subscribers worldwide
CBE for services to drama and charity (2013)

bits.t: The Devil 'Toby' Welcomes You to Hell

ranking_notes: Edge-case: Atkinson is primarily a TV/film comic. Including him here on the strength of solo live work — the Schoolmaster and the Devil are top-tier character bits — but his ranking should account for the comparatively thin volume of pure stand-up vs. his TV/film output.
